description_long: This is a dark green glass wine bottle with a tall, slender neck and a cylindrical body. The front of the bottle features a rectangular, cream-colored label with handwritten-style text and a small emblem at the top, giving it a rustic, artisanal appearance. The label is centered on the body and appears to be made of paper, with black ink for the text and graphics. The bottle has a matte finish, and the glass is opaque, preventing visibility of the contents inside. The closure at the top is not clearly visible, but the neck is long and straight, typical of wine bottles. There are no visible foil or capsule coverings on the neck, and the base of the bottle is not shown, so the punt design is unknown.
description: A dark green, matte glass wine bottle with a cream-colored, handwritten label on the front and a long, straight neck.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cream-colored label with handwritten text and emblem, centered on the bottle's body.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the bottle's cylindrical shape and the edge of the label, with the rest of the bottle unadorned.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, dark green glass surface with no label or markings visible.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view showing the bottle's curvature and a partial glimpse of the label's edge.
attribute_label_or_branding: A rectangular, cream-colored paper label with black handwritten-style text and a small emblem at the top, centered on the front of the bottle. The label covers a moderate portion of the bottle's body and is the only visible branding element.
attribute_bottle_neck_shape: Long, straight, and narrow neck with a consistent taper and no flared lip or bulge.
